summary: FastGPTMini setup.py drops and executes remote payload from anonymous file host
details: On `pip install`, setup.py fetches https://tmpfiles.org/dl/wNwdULF0K8NS/vwchmx.txt, writes the bytes to vwchmx.bat in the current directory, and executes it via `os.system("cmd /c vwchmx.bat")`. The fetched payload is opaque, has no hash or signature verification, and is hosted on an anonymous file-sharing service whose URLs are mutable and unattributable to any publisher. After the payload runs, setup.py prints a red-colored fake error claiming 'Package installation failed. Python requires administrator privileges...' to mislead the installer into thinking nothing was installed, masking the silent execution of attacker-controlled code. The package name impersonates an LLM/AI tool but ships no library code matching that purpose; its only on-install effect is the dropper. This is a classic install-time RCE dropper with social-engineering cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
